commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=051d03ba5fa21d49433a2da84d541ca49cbd3417)
commands.review_summary:
The PR hardens plugin, provider, Codex app-server, gateway startup, message-action, schema, and metadata boundaries so hostile or unreadable synthetic plugin/config/tool data is quarantined, skipped, or treated as absent instead of crashing or widening runtime decisions.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection gives a high-confidence focused reproduction for the remaining patch defect: a `Date`, `URL`, `Map`, or class instance passed to the new `describeNonJsonCompatibleValue` has no enumerable keys, so the helper returns no issue and `validateJsonSchemaValue` proceeds instead of rejecting non-JSON schema/config data.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] A narrow automated repair can address the JSON-compatibility blocker, while final merge still needs maintainer handling because the PR is draft, protected, broad, and compatibility-sensitive.

target.title: fix(plugin): harden schema and metadata fuzz boundaries
target.body:
## Summary

- Hardened runtime/plugin schema and metadata boundaries so malformed or unreadable synthetic plugin data is quarantined, omitted, or treated as absent instead of crashing assistant startup, doctor/setup flows, provider selection, channel authorization, Codex app-server routing, gateway startup planning, native approval routing, SDK helper APIs, tool policy filtering/audit, provider tool-schema compatibility traversal, embedded-agent provider schema diagnostics, plugin runtime tool factory resolution, central runtime schema projection, effective tool inventory, system-prompt diagnostics, or outbound message action handling.
- Kept tests synthetic: current fixtures use names like `fuzzplugin`, `mockplugin`, and `fuzz_move_*`, not the reported third-party plugin name.
- Latest slices guard central runtime schema projection, effective tool inventory, tool policy filtering/audit, system-prompt report construction, provider tool descriptors, embedded-agent provider tool diagnostics, and plugin factory-returned tool descriptors. Unreadable `name`, unreadable `execute`, unreadable `parameters`, normalize/inspect hook throws, unreadable thrown values, unreadable diagnostic `violations`, and unreadable tool names now omit, diagnose, audit, or log bounded warnings instead of raw-crashing schema normalization/diagnostics/tool loading/inventory/reporting.
- Earlier slices harden outbound structured attachment params, outbound JSON params (`presentation`, `delivery`, `interactive`), `runMessageAction` input param copying, exported message action normalization/target-spec helpers, exported message action threading controls/writes, native approval routing, provider wizard metadata, manifest/channel metadata, Codex image payload handling, and embedded-agent schema quarantine.

## Linked context

Which issue does this close?

No linked closing issue.

Which issues, PRs, or discussions are related?

No linked related issue.

Was this requested by a maintainer or owner?

Maintainer-directed hardening/debugging work.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: malformed runtime plugin tool schemas and hostile plugin/config/provider/manifest/native-approval/outbound-action metadata can crash or widen decisions before normal OpenClaw diagnostics, setup, provider routing, gateway startup planning, approval forwarding, message action validation, tool policy filtering/audit, provider tool-schema compatibility traversal, embedded-agent schema diagnostics, plugin runtime tool resolution, central runtime schema projection, system-prompt report construction, or Codex app-server handling complete.

Real environment tested: local focused Vitest through `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs`; broad remote validation on direct AWS Crabbox Linux runners because Blacksmith Testbox auth was unavailable in this shell.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

- Direct red/green focused Vitest repro for central runtime schema projection with unreadable synthetic tool descriptor fields (`name`, `parameters`).
- Direct red/green focused Vitest repro for effective tool inventory with unreadable synthetic tool descriptor fields (`name`, `parameters`) while preserving a healthy `exec` sibling.
- Direct red/green focused Vitest repro for tool policy filtering and audit with unreadable synthetic core/plugin tool names while preserving a healthy `exec` sibling and auditing malformed drops as `tool[index]`.
- Direct red/green focused Vitest repro for system-prompt report construction with unreadable synthetic tool descriptor fields (`name`, `parameters`) while preserving a healthy `exec` sibling.
- Direct red/green focused Vitest repro for plugin factory-returned tools with unreadable synthetic descriptor fields (`name`, `execute`, `parameters`).
- Direct pre/post `tsx` repros for embedded-agent provider normalize hook throws and unreadable provider diagnostics.
- Direct pre/post `tsx` repros for unreadable synthetic provider tool descriptors: unreadable `parameters`, unreadable `name`, and own-key traps during normalized tool copy no longer throw raw errors.
- Direct pre/post `tsx` repros for synthetic message action threading writes, unreadable threading controls, message action normalization params, runner params, provider wizard metadata, manifest channel metadata, native approval approver iterators, shared action params, structured attachments, and JSON action params.
- `CI=true OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 OPENCLAW_VITEST_FS_MODULE_CACHE_PATH=.tmp/vitest-tool-schema-projection-c009 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/tool-schema-projection.test.ts --reporter=verbose`
- `CI=true OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 OPENCLAW_VITEST_FS_MODULE_CACHE_PATH=.tmp/vitest-active-tool-schema-warnings-c009 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/commands/doctor/shared/active-tool-schema-warnings.test.ts --reporter=verbose`
- `CI=true OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 OPENCLAW_VITEST_FS_MODULE_CACHE_PATH=.tmp/vitest-tools-effective-inventory-post-rebase node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/tools-effective-inventory.test.ts --reporter=verbose`
- `CI=true OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 OPENCLAW_VITEST_FS_MODULE_CACHE_PATH=.tmp/vitest-tool-policy-pipeline-post-rebase node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/tool-policy-pipeline.test.ts src/agents/tool-policy.test.ts src/agents/tool-policy.plugin-only-allowlist.test.ts --reporter=verbose`
- `CI=true OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 OPENCLAW_VITEST_FS_MODULE_CACHE_PATH=.tmp/vitest-system-prompt-report-post-rebase-2dcc node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/system-prompt-report.test.ts --reporter=verbose`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/plugins/tools.optional.test.ts --reporter=verbose`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/tool-schema-runtime.test.ts --reporter=verbose`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/plugin-sdk/provider-tools.test.ts --reporter=verbose`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/infra/outbound/message-action-runner.threading.test.ts --reporter=verbose`
- Earlier focused batches for outbound normalization/params, shared action params, provider auth/onboarding, manifest/tool availability, channel/config fixtures, Codex image payloads, and fuzz guard type/lint drift.
- `node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check` and `node_modules/.bin/oxlint` on the latest touched file sets.
- `git diff --check`
- Added-line scrub for reported plugin names, local paths, and private network literals.
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local --base refs/remotes/origin/main`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base refs/remotes/origin/main`
- `node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run --idle-timeout 90m --ttl 240m --timing-json --shell -- "pnpm check:changed"`

Evidence after fix:

- Latest local autoreview on the tool policy descriptor slice reported no accepted/actionable findings after addressing its audit under-count finding.
- Latest focused proof after rebase to `47e86bc1ac`: `tool-policy-pipeline.test.ts`, `tool-policy.test.ts`, and `tool-policy.plugin-only-allowlist.test.ts` passed 4 Vitest projects / 67 tests; oxfmt, oxlint, `git diff --check`, and added-line scrub passed.
- System-prompt report slice proof after rebase to `2dcca3ec8a`: `system-prompt-report.test.ts` passed 1 file / 11 tests; oxfmt, oxlint, `git diff --check`, and added-line scrub passed.
- Effective inventory slice proof after rebase to `60392a1136`: `tools-effective-inventory.test.ts` passed 2 Vitest projects / 38 tests; oxfmt, oxlint, `git diff --check`, and added-line scrub passed.
- Central schema projection slice proof after rebase to `c0094a232d`: `tool-schema-projection.test.ts` passed 1 file / 7 tests; `active-tool-schema-warnings.test.ts` passed 1 file / 6 tests; oxfmt, oxlint, `git diff --check`, and added-line scrub passed.
- Plugin runtime tool descriptor slice proof: `tools.optional.test.ts` passed 1 file / 62 tests; oxfmt, oxlint, `git diff --check`, added-line scrub, and local autoreview passed.
- Embedded-agent provider diagnostics slice proof: `tool-schema-runtime.test.ts` passed 2 files / 12 tests; oxfmt, oxlint, `git diff --check`, added-line scrub, and local autoreview passed after fixing the guarded `formatError` fallback.
- Provider-tool descriptor slice proof: `provider-tools.test.ts` passed 1 file / 15 tests; oxfmt, oxlint, `git diff --check`, added-line scrub, and local autoreview passed.
- Message threading writes slice proof: `message-action-runner.threading.test.ts` passed 1 file / 24 tests; oxfmt, oxlint, `git diff --check`, added-line scrub, and local autoreview passed.
- `origin/main` advanced repeatedly with no current-slice overlap through `1901b832eb`, `3a20a0cd4f`, `5cccfe1c17`, `c903b271cf`, `091e15139b`, `61c538e2fc`, `8bd4736f03`, `f7b0b5429e`, `7bf100b028`, `ca41fa293f`, `73cf516def`, `efc93bf282`, `b1117d9862`, `fe76bae1ed`, `9b692f0a`, `b779bdb5a`, `4835a7ecd9`, `d33c2eefce`, `c0094a232d`, `b425438a58`, `c3e629cbf4`, `60392a1136`, `6f2add2cc6`, `91df558e69`, `2dcca3ec8a`, and `47e86bc1ac`; the branch rebased cleanly through those tips, with the existing doctor-warning conflict resolved to preserve current `main` behavior and synthetic fixture names.
- AWS Crabbox changed gate after the provider-wizard slice: provider `aws`, lease `cbx_991a8c29acdf`, run `run_41811346ac17`, command `pnpm check:changed`, exit 0 on base `8e806e9125`.
- Earlier AWS Crabbox changed gate for the branch: provider `aws`, lease `cbx_52b1fe398bd3`, run `run_c85914033aaa`, command `pnpm check:changed`, exit 0 over core, coreTests, extensions, and extensionTests.
- Current branch head after rebase/push: `051d03ba5fa21d49433a2da84d541ca49cbd3417`.
- Latest rebase/proof base: `47e86bc1ac5123aa32a423e63cc7183a0223f4aa`.
- GitHub `main` advanced again after the latest push/readback to `564ccf1faafd262f70cb41045ac4ba5f39132db7`; rebase is pending for the next work slice.
- Current added-line scrub: no reported plugin name, local path, or private network literal in the branch diff.

Observed result after fix:

- Hostile synthetic config/schema/provider/manifest/native-approval/message-action/plugin-tool inputs are bounded at the relevant owner boundary instead of throwing raw getter/enumeration/serialization/spread errors.
- Central runtime schema projection reports unreadable tool descriptor fields as diagnostics and filters only affected tools, preserving healthy siblings.
- Effective tool inventory uses readable tool names for lookup and lets schema projection quarantine unreadable descriptors while preserving healthy sibling inventory entries.
- Tool policy filtering and plugin group expansion drop unreadable-name descriptors without raw-crashing, and audit logs include `tool[index]` placeholders so malformed drops remain counted.
- System-prompt report construction treats unreadable tool descriptor fields as absent, uses stable `tool[index]` fallback names for unreadable names, and still reports healthy sibling tools.
- Plugin factory-returned tools with unreadable descriptor fields are diagnosed as malformed and skipped while readable sibling tools continue to resolve.
- Embedded-agent provider schema normalization now keeps original tools when provider hooks throw, and diagnostics logging stays bounded when inspect hooks or diagnostic/tool metadata are hostile.
- Provider tool-schema compatibility traversal now guards descriptor reads and normalized descriptor copies before inspecting provider-specific schema keywords.
- Message action normalization, threading controls, threading writes, JSON params, structured attachments, and runner param copies now fail closed at the relevant boundary instead of raw-crashing on synthetic accessors.
- Provider wizard setup choices, gateway startup manifest planning, native approval routing, manifest tool availability, Codex image payload handling, and embedded-agent schema-quarantine fixtures retain their previously proven behavior.

What was not tested:

- Blacksmith Testbox was not run because `blacksmith auth login` is required in this shell; direct AWS Crabbox was used for broad remote gates.
- The latest broad Crabbox gate predates the newest outbound runner/normalization/threading/provider-tool/runtime-diagnostics/plugin-tool-descriptor/projection-descriptor/effective-inventory/system-prompt-report/tool-policy slices; those slices have focused local proof and local autoreview.
- GitHub target `main` advanced to `564ccf1faa` after the latest rebase/proof/push; no proof has been rerun against that newer tip yet.
- GitHub PR checks are queued/running on the pushed head.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No new config surface. Existing malformed/unreadable config, manifest, provider, tool descriptor, diagnostic, or action inputs now fail closed or resolve as absent at bounded reads.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes. Malformed or unreadable tool/provider/plugin/native-approval/action metadata is rejected, omitted, or quarantined earlier instead of crashing runtime paths or broadening access.

What is the highest-risk area?

Overly strict sanitation could omit a valid provider tool descriptor, plugin tool descriptor, diagnostic payload, runtime projection descriptor, or outbound action parameter.

How is that risk mitigated?

The changes preserve readable sibling entries, fail closed only for route/control/identity/idempotency-style action params in the newest outbound slices, keep unreadable payload-only fields as absent, preserve numeric alias behavior, and use focused regressions plus broad AWS `check:changed` gates from earlier branch slices.

## Current review state

What is the next action?

Review the draft PR at head `051d03ba5fa21d49433a2da84d541ca49cbd3417`, or rebase once more if reviewer/CI requires the current `main` tip.

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof?

GitHub CI is queued/running after the force-push. No local actionable autoreview findings remain. GitHub target `main` advanced after this push/readback, so the next work slice should start by rebasing from `564ccf1faa` or newer.

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?

Local autoreview findings were addressed, including Codex URL-backed image-block preservation, provider wizard receiver handling, native approval false-positive target matching, shared param-reader silent truncation, message action `topLevel`/`idempotencyKey` fail-closed handling, numeric legacy-target preservation, guarded message action threading controls, guarded message action threading writes, guarded provider tool descriptors, guarded embedded-agent provider diagnostics, guarded plugin runtime tool descriptors, guarded central schema projection descriptors, guarded effective inventory name lookup, guarded system-prompt report descriptor reads, and accurate audit counting for malformed tool-policy drops.

commands.author: MonkeyLeeT

target.title: fix(agents): enforce subagent run timeouts
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: `sessions_spawn` accepted `runTimeoutSeconds`, but a plain non-terminal `agent.wait` timeout could keep the subagent run active after the stored deadline.
- Intended outcome: explicit subagent run timeouts become terminal timeout outcomes at the stored deadline, including restored/retried runs near that deadline.
- Cleanup behavior: keep-mode parent wake can proceed while child abort is still unwinding; delete-mode cleanup stays retryable while an active child cannot yet be removed.
- Intentionally out of scope: manual kill/reap behavior remains tracked by #87445.

## Linked context

Which issue does this close?

Closes #87444

Which issues, PRs, or discussions are related?

Related #87445

Was this requested by a maintainer or owner?

Requested through the maintainer workflow for #87444.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

Behavior addressed: `sessions_spawn` child runs with explicit `runTimeoutSeconds` no longer remain active after the stored deadline when `agent.wait` only reports a non-terminal timeout; restored/retried waits now use only the remaining stored deadline budget before completing the timeout path.

Real environment tested: local OpenClaw source worktree running a real gateway/server and real `agent` RPC against a loopback OpenAI Responses-compatible provider harness. The parent model turn called `sessions_spawn`, then `sessions_yield`; the child provider request was held open past `runTimeoutSeconds: 1`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node --import tsx /private/tmp/openclaw-issue-87444-proof.mts`; `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/subagent-registry.test.ts src/agents/subagent-announce.timeout.test.ts`; `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/openclaw-tools.subagents.sessions-spawn.lifecycle.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/agent.test.ts`; `git diff --check`. After the latest deadline-before-wait amendment, reran `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/subagent-registry.test.ts src/agents/subagent-announce.timeout.test.ts`, `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/openclaw-tools.subagents.sessions-spawn.lifecycle.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/agent.test.ts`, and `git diff --check`.

Evidence after fix: copied terminal output from the real gateway proof run:

```text
ISSUE_87444_PARENT_WOKE_C7A617
ISSUE_87444_REAL_BEHAVIOR_PROOF pass
{
  "sessionKey": "agent:main:issue-87444-c7a617",
  "taskName": "proof_child_timeout",
  "runId": "6298edb3-70e5-4926-91f3-9e98ebdb4ed1",
  "runTimeoutSeconds": 1,
  "outcomeStatus": "timeout",
  "elapsedMs": 1000,
  "deadlineDeltaMs": 0,
  "activeChildrenAfterTimeout": 0,
  "parentWakeDispatchCompleted": true,
  "providerRequestLabels": [
    "parent:function_call:sessions_spawn",
    "parent:function_call:sessions_yield",
    "child:provider_request_held_open",
    "parent:message:timeout_observed"
  ],
  "childProviderLateResponseServedBeforeParentWake": false
}
ISSUE_87444_PROOF_REQUEST_LOG ["parent:function_call:sessions_spawn","parent:function_call:sessions_yield","child:provider_request_held_open","parent:message:timeout_observed"]
```

Observed result after fix: the real gateway run accepted `sessions_spawn` with `runTimeoutSeconds: 1`, the held-open child recorded `outcomeStatus: "timeout"` at exactly `elapsedMs: 1000`, `activeChildrenAfterTimeout` was `0`, and the parent continuation produced the redacted wake token `ISSUE_87444_PARENT_WOKE_C7A617`.

What was not tested: external hosted provider credentials, external channel delivery, GitHub Actions CI completion, and the separate manual kill/reap path tracked by #87445.

Proof limitations or environment constraints: the provider endpoint was a loopback deterministic OpenAI Responses-compatible harness so no API keys or external messages were used; the exercised OpenClaw path was still the real gateway, real agent RPC, real `sessions_spawn`, real `sessions_yield`, registry timeout completion, and parent wake dispatch.

Before evidence (optional but encouraged): before the original registry change, the focused regression kept `completedRun?.endedAt` undefined after a plain `agent.wait` timeout. Before the deadline-before-wait amendment, a restored run 59s into a 60s timeout still passed a full 60s budget to `agent.wait`.

## Tests and validation

Which commands did you run?

- `node --import tsx /private/tmp/openclaw-issue-87444-proof.mts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/subagent-registry.test.ts src/agents/subagent-announce.timeout.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/openclaw-tools.subagents.sessions-spawn.lifecycle.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/agent.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`

What regression coverage was added or updated?

Added registry coverage for non-terminal `agent.wait` timeouts at and near the stored explicit run deadline, restored-run coverage proving a resumed run near its deadline waits only for the remaining budget, and announce coverage proving `cleanup: "delete"` timeout cleanup remains retryable while the embedded child request is still active.

What failed before this fix, if known?

The new restored-run regression failed before the latest production change because a run restored 59s into a 60s timeout still passed `timeoutMs: 60000` to `agent.wait` instead of `timeoutMs: 1000`. ClawSweeper also found that the previous announce branch could mark delete cleanup complete while deletion was skipped.

If no test was added, why not?

N/A; focused regression tests were added.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No.

What is the highest-risk area?

Prematurely marking a still-running child as timed out if the gateway wait path returns a plain timeout before the configured child deadline, or finalizing delete cleanup while the child is still active.

How is that risk mitigated?

Fresh subagent waits keep the existing initial wait budget. Resumed/retried waits are capped to the stored deadline, session-store reconciliation still wins before synthetic timeout completion, and focused tests cover pre-deadline retry, boundary timeout completion, restored near-deadline wait capping, and delete-cleanup retryability.

## Current review state

What is the next action?

Wait for GitHub CI and ClawSweeper re-review on the updated head/body.

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof?

GitHub CI has not completed on the latest forced-pushed head; external hosted provider/channel proof was not run.

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?

Addressed ClawSweeper’s deadline-before-wait finding, real-behavior proof request, and cleanup-delete finding.
